title: Migrate IMDb data layer from cinemagoer to OMDb+TMDb hybrid
description:
**Background**: cinemagoer is no longer viable - IMDb pages are now behind AWS WAF JS challenge (see MoLiM-5tz). HTTP scraping cannot recover. Must switch to proper JSON APIs.

**Hybrid strategy**:
- **OMDb API** -> IMDb rating (imdbRating) + IMDb vote count (imdbVotes). Keeps existing rating-based folder convention and Data/*.txt files semantically valid.
- **TMDb API** -> richer/cleaner metadata: cast, crew (directors, writers, producers), runtime, genres, posters, release dates, episode lists for series.
- Glue: both APIs accept/return the IMDb tt-ID, so a single tt-ID per movie ties responses together.

**Why hybrid (not OMDb alone)**:
- OMDb is one maintainer's hobby project (single point of failure), 1000 req/day free tier, scrapes IMDb so could break similarly to cinemagoer.
- TMDb is a stable production API with much higher quotas, cleaner data, but has its own rating (not IMDb) and weaker rating-population for older/obscure titles.
- Hybrid gives us the IMDb numbers our historical data depends on AND robust metadata.

**Why not TMDb alone**:
- TMDb's vote_average is its own rating (~8.2 for Matrix vs IMDb 8.7), incompatible with existing Data/Directors rating.txt, Data/Actors rating.txt, and the underscore-prefix folder system that encodes IMDb ratings.

**Out of scope here**: GUI changes, database migration, business-logic refactor (those are separate phases). This epic strictly replaces the data-fetching layer behind the existing call sites.

title: Create ProcessingService for folder operations
description: Extract folder processing logic into ProcessingService. Methods: process_folder(), rename_by_rating(), extract_metadata_from_folder().

title: Create Series/Season/Episode dataclasses
description: Create molim/core/models/series.py with Series, Season, and Episode dataclasses for TV series management.

title: Add OMDb + TMDb API keys to config and document setup
description:
**Tasks**:
- Add a config mechanism (env vars or .env file via python-dotenv) for OMDB_API_KEY and TMDB_API_KEY
- Add example .env.example committed to repo
- Add .env to .gitignore
- Update README.md and docs/ with signup links and setup steps
- Update setup.ps1 / setup.sh to prompt for / verify keys
- Pin requirements: requests (replace cinemagoer dependency) and python-dotenv

**Acceptance**: fresh clone -> setup.ps1 -> user prompted/instructed for keys -> keys readable from code.
notes:
DELIVERED:
  - config.py: get_settings() with @lru_cache; .env via python-dotenv;
    Settings.require_api_keys() raises ConfigError listing missing keys
  - .env.example committed; .env gitignored (verified: .gitignore line 85)
  - requirements.txt: added requests>=2.32,<3 and python-dotenv>=1.0,<2;
    cinemagoer pinned with deprecation comment (removal scheduled in MoLiM-dyy)
  - setup.ps1 + setup.sh: copy .env.example -> .env if missing, list signup
    URLs, run config.py to verify keys, mention 3.11 lock will lift in dyy
  - README.md: new "API Keys" section with table of OMDb/TMDb signup links,
    .env layout, TMDb attribution disclaimer

VERIFIED:
  - python config.py -> "OK - both keys present"
  - get_errors on config.py -> clean

DECISION: cinemagoer kept in requirements until MoLiM-dyy because imdbAccess.py
still imports it; removing now would break the existing tree mid-migration.

UNBLOCKS: MoLiM-6w5 (OMDbClient), MoLiM-4rr (TMDbClient).

title: Phase 4: Modern CLI with Click
description: Replace procedural scripts with proper Click-based CLI. Create commands for processing, statistics, search, and database management with progress bars and colored output.

title: Implement 'molim search' command
description: Create search command with filters: --director, --actor, --genre, --rating-min, --year. Display results in formatted table.

title: Phase 0: Testing Infrastructure & Verification
description: Create baseline tests before refactoring begins. Use TestData/ folder with sample movies and series to verify IMDb fetching, data parsing, and folder operations work correctly. Essential for ensuring refactoring doesn't break existing functionality.

title: Create IMDbService for API interactions
description: Create molim/core/services/imdb_service.py wrapping cinemagoer API. Include rate limiting, error handling, and retry logic. Methods: fetch_movie(), fetch_series(), search().

title: Create test_imdb_fetching.py for IMDb API
description: Test imdbAccess.py functions: fetchMovieData(), fetchSeriesData(). Use TestData/ folder samples. Verify: correct movie/series identification, metadata extraction (title, year, rating, cast, directors), proper error handling.
notes: RESOLVED: Switched from Python 3.14 to 3.11. All 8 IMDb tests now PASSING (previously skipped). cinemagoer works perfectly with Python 3.11.

title: Spike: evaluate OMDb + TMDb APIs for MoLiM data needs
description:
**Goal**: Validate the hybrid approach with real calls before writing any production code.

**Deliverables**:
1. Sign up for OMDb API key (https://omdbapi.com) - free tier, 1000/day
2. Sign up for TMDb API key (https://developer.themoviedb.org) - free, requires attribution
3. One-off script that, given a movie folder name, fetches:
   - OMDb: title, year, imdbRating, imdbVotes, imdbID, Type
   - TMDb (by imdb_id): cast (top 10), directors, writers, producers, runtime, genres
4. Verify all fields MoLiM currently uses are obtainable.
5. Verify series/episode flow works (TMDb tv endpoints + external_ids).
6. Document any gaps in a comment on this issue (e.g. fields one API has and the other doesn't).

**Acceptance**: working script run against 5+ titles from TestData/ producing the same shape of data imdbAccess.py used to produce.
notes:
SPIKE COMPLETE - hybrid validated end-to-end.

Re-ran spike/spike_omdb_tmdb.py with BOTH keys against TestData/Movies and
TestData/Series. All required IMDBMovieData fields recovered.

Confirmed fields per source:
  OMDb (ratings layer):
    rating  -> imdbRating (e.g. F1 7.6, HoD 8.3)
    votes   -> imdbVotes (parsed from comma-formatted string)
    box_office -> "BoxOffice" (e.g. "$279,989,632")
    movieID -> imdbID (canonical)
    year, releaseDate, countries, languages, plot, runtime fallback

  TMDb (rich-content layer):
    cast_complete, cast_leads, directors, writers, producers
    genres, cover_url (poster), runtime
    For series: number_of_seasons, number_of_episodes, episode_run_time

CONFIRMED PERMANENT GAP:
  top250rank - IMDb-internal, not exposed by either API. Acceptable - minor
  display field, can be dropped or recomputed locally from rating threshold.

OBSERVATIONS:
  - OMDb releaseDate format is "27 Jun 2025"; TMDb is "2025-06-25" - normalize
    in MovieDataService to ISO.
  - TMDb writers contains dupes when same person credited multiple times -
    dedupe in client.
  - Series releaseDate (TMDb first_air_date) more reliable than OMDb "Year"
    string ("2022-").
  - OMDb response is fast (~200ms); TMDb /find + /movie is two calls (~600ms
    total). Cache layer in MovieDataService is essential.

VERDICT: Hybrid OMDb + TMDb fully replaces cinemagoer. Proceed with:
  - MoLiM-1n6 (config + .env loader)
  - MoLiM-6w5 (OMDbClient)
  - MoLiM-4rr (TMDbClient)

Spike artifacts retained:
  spike/spike_omdb_tmdb.py
  spike/spike_run_1.txt   (TMDb-only)
  spike/spike_run_2.txt   (full hybrid)

title: Implement TMDbClient (metadata + cast/crew by IMDb ID)
description:
**Module**: new file molim_data/tmdb_client.py

**Responsibilities**:
- Auth via Bearer token (v4) or api_key query param (v3) - pick one and document
- Methods:
  - find_by_imdb_id(tt_id) -> {movie_id|tv_id}
  - get_movie(tmdb_id, append='credits') -> normalized dict
  - get_tv(tmdb_id, append='credits,external_ids') -> normalized dict
  - get_tv_season(tmdb_id, season_number)
- Normalize cast: top N actors with character names
- Normalize crew: split into directors / writers / producers by job field
- Map TMDb genre IDs -> names (cache /genre/movie/list once)
- Same retry/timeout/logging discipline as OMDbClient

**Acceptance**: unit tests cover movie + tv flows including cast/crew split and genre mapping.

title: Design SQLite database schema
description: Create schema.sql with tables: movies, series, people, genres, movie_people, movie_genres. Include indexes and foreign keys.

title: Setup pytest infrastructure for legacy code
description: Install pytest, pytest-cov, pytest-mock. Create tests/ directory with conftest.py for fixtures. Configure pytest.ini for test discovery. Create TestData fixtures for sample movies/series.

title: Create database connection manager
description: Create molim/infrastructure/database/connection.py with singleton DatabaseConnection class, connection pooling, and context manager support.

title: Cinemagoer 2025.5.19 has critical parser bugs
description:
**Problem**: cinemagoer development version (2025.5.19 from GitHub) has multiple parser bugs due to IMDb website redesign.

**Affected Data**:
- Cast: UNAVAILABLE (parser doesn't extract from new IMDb design)
- Runtime: UNAVAILABLE (parser doesn't extract)
- Producers: UNAVAILABLE (parser doesn't extract)
- Writers: UNAVAILABLE (parser doesn't extract)  
- Votes: Returns inflated counts by 10x (e.g., 22M instead of 2.2M)

**Working Data**:
- Title, Year, Rating, Directors, Genres (all work correctly)
- Votes (only with 'vote details' info set, but inflated 10x)

**Root Cause**: IMDb redesigned website, cinemagoer parsers incomplete/buggy

**Workarounds Implemented**:
1. Graceful degradation: code handles missing data with 'UNAVAILABLE' markers
2. Vote inflation fix: divide by 10 when votes > 10M
3. Clear logging: shows 'cinemagoer parser issue' messages

**Options**:
1. Wait for cinemagoer maintainers to fix (unknown timeline)
2. Switch to alternative API (TMDb, OMDb)
3. Debug/fix parsers ourselves and submit PR
4. Continue with partial data (current approach)

**Status**: Workarounds in place, application functional with degraded data
notes:
UPDATE 2026-05-07: Re-investigated after 6 months. Situation is WORSE, not better.

UPSTREAM PROGRESS:
- cinemagoer master HEAD has commit 'version 2025.12.31' (aca6269)
- Multiple parser rewrites merged Nov 9-Dec 26 2025 ('Fix parsers for current IMDB HTML structure')
- BUT no PyPI release; PyPI still pinned at 2023.5.1
- Upstream issues #555, #554, #546 all open with same symptoms

NEW ROOT CAUSE (more fundamental):
- IMDb now sits behind AWS WAF bot challenge (challenge.js)
- HTTP GET to imdb.com/title/tt*/reference/ returns 202 + 1991-byte JS challenge page
- Body contains 'awsWafCookieDomainList', 'AwsWafIntegration.saveReferrer()'
- HTML for the actual page is NEVER served to non-browser clients
- Tested with both default and full Chrome User-Agent headers - same result
- Therefore NO parser fix in cinemagoer can recover the data; it never arrives

REPRODUCTION:
  python -c "import urllib.request as u; r=u.urlopen(u.Request('\''https://www.imdb.com/title/tt0133093/reference/'\'', headers={'\''User-Agent'\'':'\''Mozilla/5.0 ... Chrome/131'\''})); print(r.status, len(r.read()))"
  -> 202 1991

CONCLUSION:
- Option 1 (wait for cinemagoer fix) is no longer viable - the upstream cannot fix this without adding a JS-challenge solver (headless browser, Playwright)
- Option 2 (switch API) is now the recommended path:
    * OMDb API (free tier, JSON, requires API key) - simplest drop-in
    * TMDb API (richer data, JSON, free with attribution)
- Option 3 (continue cinemagoer with workarounds) is no longer functional - all fields return None now, not just some

RECOMMENDED ACTION: File a new task to migrate IMDb data layer to OMDb or TMDb.

title: Implement Library browser tab
description: Create TreeView for movie library with columns (Name, Year, Rating, Director). Add search/filter panel and detail view.

title: Implement OMDbClient (ratings + votes by IMDb ID)
description:
**Module**: new file molim_data/omdb_client.py (or wherever fits the layout)

**Responsibilities**:
- Wrap OMDb /?i=tt... endpoint with requests
- Methods: get_by_imdb_id(tt_id), search(title, year=None) -> tt_id
- Parse imdbRating (str -> float), imdbVotes (str with commas -> int)
- Return a typed dict / dataclass: OmdbResult(imdb_id, title, year, imdb_rating, imdb_votes, type, raw)
- Handle: 401 (bad key), 'Response: False' error payload, network errors, rate limit (429)
- Rate limiting: simple sleep(0.1) between calls (well under 1000/day)
- Retry with backoff on 5xx/timeout (max 3)
- Logging at INFO/WARN/ERROR levels - no print()

**Acceptance**: unit tests with mocked requests cover: success, not-found, bad-key, malformed JSON, retry-on-5xx.

title: Update IMDb-fetching tests for OMDb/TMDb mocks
description:
**Tasks**:
- Rewrite tests/test_imdb_fetching.py to mock OMDbClient + TMDbClient (not cinemagoer)
- Add fixtures with realistic OMDb/TMDb JSON payloads (anonymized) under tests/fixtures/
- Cover: movie fetch, series fetch, cache hit, cache miss, missing rating, 404, rate-limit retry
- Aim to keep coverage >= current level

**Acceptance**: pytest -q passes; coverage on new client/service modules >= 80%.

title: Phase 6: Testing & Quality Assurance
description: Add comprehensive test suite with pytest. Create unit tests for all services and repositories, integration tests for database operations, and end-to-end tests for CLI commands.

title: Create test_folder_operations.py
description: Test fileOperations.py functions: getMovieNameFromFolder(), extractYearFromString(), folder name parsing. Use TestData/ samples to verify correct extraction.

title: Write unit tests for repositories
description: Create tests/unit/test_repositories.py with tests for MovieRepository, SeriesRepository CRUD operations using in-memory SQLite.

title: Create migration script from text files to database
description: Create scripts/migrate_to_db.py to read all existing 'Film data - *.txt' files and populate SQLite database. Include progress reporting and error handling.

title: Implement MovieDataService - orchestrates OMDb + TMDb hybrid lookup
description:
**Module**: new file molim_data/movie_data_service.py

**Flow for a movie**:
1. Search OMDb by (title, year) -> imdbID + imdbRating + imdbVotes
2. find_by_imdb_id on TMDb -> tmdb_id
3. get_movie(tmdb_id, append='credits') -> cast/crew/runtime/genres
4. Merge into single MovieData object matching the contract that imdbAccess.fetchMovieData() exposed today (so call sites need minimal changes)

**Series flow**: same idea using OMDb Type=series and TMDb /tv endpoints.

**Caching**: simple JSON-file cache keyed by tt-ID (to avoid re-fetching during stats/reports runs and to stay under quotas). Cache TTL configurable (default 30 days).

**Failure modes**: if OMDb has no rating but TMDb has data, return MovieData with rating=None and clear logging. Never crash; always return something the caller can handle.

**Acceptance**: integration test (with cassette/VCR or stubbed clients) producing identical MovieData shape for at least one movie + one series sample from TestData/.

title: Implement Processing tab
description: Create processing tab with folder selection, progress bar, and log output. Allow batch processing of new movies/series.

title: Phase 3: Business Logic Layer
description: Extract business logic into service layer. Create IMDbService, ProcessingService, and StatisticsService. Implement proper separation between data access and business rules.

title: Replace imdbAccess.py call sites with MovieDataService
description:
**Tasks**:
- Identify all current imports/uses of imdbAccess (FolderWithMovies.py, processing.py, movieStatistics.py, MoLiM.py, etc.)
- Swap calls: ia.get_movie/search_movie -> MovieDataService methods
- Update IMDBMovieData/IMDBSeriesData/IMDBEpisodeData/IMDBSeriesSeasonData to be populated from new dicts (keep their public API stable if possible)
- Remove 'UNAVAILABLE (cinemagoer parser issue)' workaround branches
- Remove vote-inflation /10 hack
- Delete cinemagoer from requirements.txt

**Acceptance**: full app run on TestData/ folder produces correct, complete data; all existing tests pass.

title: Create test_processing_pipeline.py
description: Test processing.py end-to-end: processFolder() with TestData/ samples. Verify: folder scanning, IMDb lookup, data extraction, file writing. Mock IMDb API to avoid rate limits in tests.

title: Add CI-friendly test markers and mocking
description: Add pytest markers for @pytest.mark.slow (real IMDb API), @pytest.mark.unit (mocked). Create mock responses for TestData samples. Enable running tests without internet/API access.

title: Decommission cinemagoer: remove imports and update docs
description:
**Tasks**:
- Remove 'from imdb import Cinemagoer' / 'ia = Cinemagoer()' from imdbAccess.py and movieStatistics.py
- Either delete imdbAccess.py or shrink it to a thin compat shim that delegates to MovieDataService (for any external callers)
- Update OVERVIEW.md to reflect new data layer
- Update REFACTORING_PLAN.md (data-fetching now handled in phase0)
- Update QUICK_START.md
- Update docs/PYTHON_VERSION.md - the cinemagoer/Python 3.11 constraint is gone! Likely free to move to 3.12+/3.13
- Close MoLiM-5tz with a note pointing at this epic

**Acceptance**: 'rg cinemagoer' in repo returns no production hits.

title: Fix invalid escape sequences in myFolders.py
description: Convert all string paths to use raw strings (r'') or double backslashes to fix Python warnings about invalid escape sequences. Files: myFolders.py, MoLiM.py

title: Write unit tests for services
description: Create tests/unit/test_services.py with mocked tests for IMDbService, ProcessingService, StatisticsService.

title: Implement 'molim process' command
description: Create process command with options: --folder, --recursive, --dry-run. Include progress bar for batch processing.

title: Create test fixtures with known good data
description: Document expected results for TestData/ samples: correct titles, years, IMDb IDs. Create JSON fixtures with expected metadata. This serves as regression baseline.

title: Create config.yaml configuration system
description: Create config.yaml with all paths, IMDb settings, rating thresholds, and logging configuration. Implement Config singleton class with YAML loading and dot notation access.

title: Implement 'molim stats' command group
description: Create stats subcommands: directors, actors, genres, ratings. Include --format (table/json/csv) and --export options.

title: Write integration tests for database
description: Create tests/integration/test_database.py testing full database operations: migrations, complex queries, transactions.

title: Implement BaseRepository with CRUD operations
description: Create molim/core/repositories/base.py with generic BaseRepository class providing get(), save(), delete(), find_all() methods.

title: Setup Click CLI framework
description: Install Click and create molim/ui/cli/commands.py with main command group. Setup entry point in setup.py for 'molim' command.

title: Phase 1: Foundation & Cleanup
description: Fix immediate issues, create configuration system, setup logging, and reorganize project structure. This is the critical foundation for all subsequent refactoring work.

title: Reorganize into molim/ package structure
description: Create new directory structure: molim/core/, molim/infrastructure/, molim/ui/, molim/utils/. Move old files to legacy/ folder. Create all __init__.py files and setup.py.

title: Create Movie dataclass with validation
description: Create molim/core/models/movie.py with Movie dataclass. Include all fields (id, name, imdb_id, year, rating, genres, directors, cast, etc.) and methods (is_directed_by, has_genre, get_rating_category).

title: Phase 5: Tkinter GUI Implementation
description: Build desktop GUI using Tkinter with ttk widgets. Create main window with tabs for library browsing, statistics, processing, and settings. Implement MVVM pattern for separation.

title: Implement Statistics tab with charts
description: Create statistics tab with matplotlib charts: rating distribution, genre breakdown, top directors/actors. Add export options.

title: Setup pytest test framework
description: Install pytest, pytest-cov, pytest-mock. Create tests/ directory structure with unit/, integration/, and fixtures/. Setup pytest.ini and tox.ini.

title: Create StatisticsService for analytics
description: Create StatisticsService with methods: get_director_stats(), get_actor_stats(), get_genre_distribution(), get_rating_distribution().

title: Setup logging system with rotation
description: Create logging_config.py with console and rotating file handlers. Replace all print() statements with proper logging (logger.info, logger.error, etc.)

title: Remove commented-out code from MoLiM.py
description: Clean up MoLiM.py by removing all commented-out function calls and old code. Keep only active, working code.

title: Implement MovieRepository
description: Create movie_repository.py extending BaseRepository with movie-specific queries: find_by_director(), find_by_rating_range(), find_by_genre().

title: Design main window with menu and tabs
description: Create molim/ui/gui/main_window.py with menu bar, toolbar, and ttk.Notebook for tabs (Library, Statistics, Processing, Settings).

title: Phase 2: Data Layer Migration
description: Migrate from text file storage to SQLite database. Create modern data models with dataclasses, design database schema, implement Repository pattern, and migrate existing data.
